2605.02290.title: Distilling Long-CoT Reasoning through Collaborative Step-wise Multi-Teacher Decoding
2605.02290.paper_summary: Distilling large reasoning models is essential for making Long-CoT reasoning practical, as full-scale inference remains computationally prohibitive. Existing curation-based approaches select complete reasoning traces post-hoc, overlooking collaboration among heterogeneous teachers and lacking dynamic exploration, which leads to redundant sampling and missed complementary reasoning. We introduce CoRD, a collaborative multi-teacher decoding framework that performs step-wise reasoning synthesis guided by predictive perplexity-based scoring and beam search. This enables heterogeneous LRMs to jointly construct coherent reasoning trajectories while efficiently preserving diverse, high-potential hypotheses. Experiments show that CoRD produces higher-quality reasoning data and achieves near teacher-level student performance with fewer, structured supervision signals, without substantial efficiency overhead. CoRD further generalizes well to out-of-domain and open-ended settings. The dataset and model are available at \href{https://github.com/DISL-Lab/CoRD}{https://github.com/DISL-Lab/CoRD}.
2605.02290.paper_summary_zh: 提煉大型推理模型對於使長期鏈式推理實用至關重要，因為全規模推理在計算上仍然是不可行的。現有的基於策展的方法在事後選擇完整的推理痕跡，忽略了異質教師之間的合作，並缺乏動態探索，這導致了冗餘的抽樣和錯過互補推理。我們介紹了CoRD，一個協作多教師解碼框架，通過基於預測困惑度的評分和束搜索進行逐步推理綜合。這使得異質的LRMs能夠共同構建連貫的推理軌跡，同時有效地保留多樣化的高潛力假設。實驗表明，CoRD產生了更高質量的推理數據，並在較少的結構化監督信號下達到了接近教師水平的學生表現，且沒有顯著的效率開銷。CoRD在域外和開放式設置中進一步具有良好的泛化能力。數據集和模型可在 \href{https://github.com/DISL-Lab/CoRD}{https://github.com/DISL-Lab/CoRD} 獲得。
2605.02290.author: Taewon Yun et.al.
2605.02290.authors: Taewon Yun, Jisu Shin, Jeonghwan Choi, Seunghwan Bang, Hwanjun Song
